DEFENDANT STACEY SOLOMAN'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16 (ANTI-SLAPP MOTION)
Off Calendar Aug-29-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Michael Wright; 628-688-7176; non-party, appearing as representative for plaintiff Sharon Allen.
Candice N. Hamant; Tyson & Mendes LLP; 628-253-5070; appearing as counsel for defendant Stacey Soloman.

Mr. Wright appears and attempts to argue on behalf of plaintiff. Mr. Wright did not see the court's tentative ruling until the morning of the hearing, nor did he give notice to opposing counsel between the hours of 3-4pm on 8/28/2023 that he or plaintiff would be appearing in court today to contest the court's tentative ruling. The court explains to Mr. Wright that because he is not an attorney, he cannot appear or argue on behalf of plaintiff. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STACEY SOLOMAN'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16 (ANTI-SLAPP MOTION) is GRANTED. Moving party met its burden under the first prong. On August 25, 2023 Plaintiff filed documents, which may be construed as an opposition. It is not clear if these documents have been served. This opposition would be untimely and does not appear to contain argument or evidence that would meet the requirements under the second prong of the Anti-Slapp statute.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Michael Wright; 628-688-7176; non-party, appearing as representative for plaintiff Sharon Allen.
Candice N. Hamant; Tyson & Mendes LLP; 628-253-5070; appearing as counsel for defendant Stacey Soloman.

Mr. Wright appears and attempts to argue on behalf of plaintiff. Mr. Wright did not see the court's tentative ruling until the morning of the hearing, nor did he give notice to opposing counsel between the hours of 3-4pm on 8/28/2023 that he or plaintiff would be appearing in court today to contest the court's tentative ruling. The court explains to Mr. Wright that because he is not an attorney, he cannot appear or argue on behalf of plaintiff. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS RELATED MANAGEMENT COMPANY, LP AND LA SALLE PRESERVATION, LP'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to file a complaint asserting and clearly labeling legally cognizable claims against the moving defendants. Plaintiff shall have 30 days from the notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. All parts of the amended complaint must be in clear and concise language and must be legible.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
